commit.message:
refactor(query): refactor flight shuffle (#19458)

* feat(query): add ExecutorWaker to wake up processors from outside executor

Add ExecutorWaker mechanism that allows processors to be woken up from
external code. This enables external events to trigger processor scheduling
in the pipeline executor.

- Add ExecutorWaker struct with OnceLock-based callback binding
- Pipeline creates waker automatically and exposes via get_waker()
- QueryPipelineExecutor binds waker callback in try_create()
- QueriesPipelineExecutor binds waker for each graph in send_graph()
- Support multiple pipelines by proxying individual wakers to graph-level waker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(query): add PingPongExchange for non-blocking flight data exchange

Add a ping-pong style flight exchange that guarantees at most one request
is in-flight at any time. This enables efficient round-trip time measurement
and flow control for flight data exchange operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* z

* feat(query): add ExchangeSinkBuffer for backpressure control in distributed exchange

- Add ExchangeSinkBuffer with ping-pong mode for at most one in-flight request per destination
- Use Vec instead of HashMap for pre-allocated channels and remote instances
- Use ConcurrentQueue for lock-free wake target collection
- Add WakeTarget with Arc and AtomicBool for efficient backpressure wake-up
- Simplify PingPongCallback trait by removing exchange parameter
- Add force_send and ready_send methods to PingPongExchange

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(query): add ExchangeChannel trait and BroadcastChannel for distributed exchange

Add ExchangeChannel trait as unified interface for local/remote data exchange.
Implement RemoteChannel for serializing DataBlock to FlightData via Arrow IPC.
Add BroadcastChannel for round-robin distribution across multiple channels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(query): add ThreadChannel for do_exchange server-side with per-connection backpressure

Introduces a thread-channel based exchange buffer for the do_exchange server side.
Each network connection gets per-connection memory quota with backpressure via
event-listener. Processors prioritize consuming from the connection with highest
memory usage. ThreadChannelReader is a pure sync processor using FlaggedWaker +
RecvFuture (async_channel's try-listen-retry pattern). Also adds Processor::on_id_set
for deferred waker creation, ExchangeChannel::poll_send API, and do_exchange RPC handler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): move ThreadChannel to network/inbound_channel and rename to NetworkInbound*

Move the do_exchange server-side inbound channel from exchange/ to a new
network/ module, since it is a network-layer component. Split structs into
separate files (inbound_channel.rs, inbound_quota.rs) for clarity.

Type renames:
- ThreadChannel → NetworkInboundChannel
- ThreadChannelSet → NetworkInboundChannelSet
- ThreadChannelSender → NetworkInboundSender
- ThreadChannelReceiver → NetworkInboundReceiver

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): move exchange_channel, exchange_sink_buffer, ping_pong_exchange to network/

Move outbound network components from exchange/ to network/ module:
- exchange_channel.rs → network/outbound_channel.rs
- exchange_sink_buffer.rs → network/outbound_buffer.rs
- ping_pong_exchange.rs → network/ping_pong_exchange.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): rename network/ping_pong_exchange to network/outbound_transport

Aligns naming with outbound_buffer/outbound_channel to form a clear layering:
outbound_channel → outbound_buffer → outbound_transport

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): change ExchangeChannel::poll_send to add_block returning BoxFuture

Replace the poll-based interface with an async interface for simpler usage
by callers. The poll_fn bridge in RemoteChannel preserves the existing
backpressure semantics of ExchangeSinkBuffer::poll_send.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): add InboundChannel trait and rename ExchangeChannel to OutboundChannel

- Add InboundChannel trait with recv()/close() methods, implemented by
  NetworkInboundReceiver
- Rename ExchangeChannel to OutboundChannel for symmetry
- Update ThreadChannelReader to use Arc<dyn InboundChannel> instead of
  concrete NetworkInboundReceiver
- RecvFuture now holds Arc<NetworkInboundChannel> directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): add InboundChannel/OutboundChannel traits, update exchange_manager

- InboundChannel trait: recv() -> BoxFuture, close()
- OutboundChannel trait (renamed from ExchangeChannel): add_block() -> BoxFuture
- NetworkInboundReceiver implements InboundChannel
- ThreadChannelReader uses Arc<dyn InboundChannel>
- exchange_manager::get_exchange_source_channel returns Arc<dyn InboundChannel>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(query): add ThreadChannelWriter sink processor using OutboundChannel

Pure synchronous sink processor (no async_process) that sends DataBlocks
through an OutboundChannel. Uses FlaggedWaker to poll the BoxFuture from
add_block, matching the same pattern as ThreadChannelReader.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): extract FlaggedWaker to network/ as shared utility

Deduplicate FlaggedWaker from ThreadChannelReader and ThreadChannelWriter
into network/flagged_waker.rs. Both processors now use FlaggedWaker::create()
to wrap the executor waker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): make RecvFuture private and update module docs

RecvFuture is now an implementation detail of NetworkInboundReceiver,
no longer exposed through the InboundChannel trait.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(query): pass num_threads in do_exchange, fix tid routing, add DataExchange::get_id

- Add DoExchangeParams struct (query_id, exchange_id, num_threads) serialized
  as JSON in x-exchange-params gRPC metadata, replacing individual metadata fields
- Server uses num_threads from coordinator to create NetworkInboundChannelSet
  with correct size, eliminating race condition with max_threads fallback
- Fix tid routing: RemoteChannel prepends tid as 2-byte u16 LE to app_metadata
  matching extract_tid(), instead of unused FlightDescriptor.cmd encoding
- One PingPong per remote node with N tids multiplexed; strip_tid in receiver
- Edge::ExchangeFragment now carries exchange_id + channels per node pair
- Add DataExchange::get_id() with GlobalUniq id on all exchange variants
- Rename num_channels to num_threads throughout for clarity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* z

* fix(query): coalesce FlaggedWaker wakes, fix backpressure race conditions, add tests

- FlaggedWaker now uses swap(true) to coalesce multiple wakes into one
  executor scheduling. reset() at event() start re-enables the next wake.
- Fix backpressure race in ExchangeSinkBuffer::add_data and
  LocalOutboundChannel::add_block: use try-register-retry pattern to
  re-check condition after waker registration, preventing missed wakeups.
- Add 7 unit tests for FlaggedWaker (coalescing, reset, clone sharing,
  concurrent wakes with barrier, concurrent wake-reset cycles).
- Add 7 unit tests for LocalChannel (send/recv, close, backpressure
  trigger, no missed wakeup, concurrent send/recv, multi-channel).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* z

* z

* z

* z

* z

* z

* z

* Remove per-block schema from network exchange FlightData

Schema was being serialized into every FlightData's app_metadata on the
send side and deserialized on the receive side. This is redundant since
the schema is known from the exchange plan params.

Now NetworkInboundReceiver stores the schema at creation time (injected
from BroadcastExchangeParams) and uses it for all deserialization,
matching the pattern already used by TransformExchangeDeserializer.
Supports override_block_schema() for dynamic schema cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* z

* z

* Rename BroadcastChannel to RoundRobinChannel

The struct round-robins blocks across per-thread channels on a single
remote node. The broadcast semantics live in BroadcastSendTransform,
not here. Name it for what it actually does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Batch merge/split for FlightData in network exchange

Merge multiple small FlightData items into a single batch (up to
max_batch_bytes) on the outbound side, and split them back on the
inbound side using zero-copy Bytes::split_to(). This reduces RTT
overhead when exchanging many small blocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* z

* z

* z

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(query): tolerate unknown TaggableObject variants in tag reference listing (#19497)

fix(meta): tolerate unknown TaggableObject variants in tag reference listing

  When a newer version writes tag references for object types unknown to
  the current binary (e.g. UDF, Procedure), `list_pb` fails on key decode
  and aborts the entire operation.

  Replace `list_pb` + `try_collect` with `list_kv` + manual decode in
  `drop_tag` and `list_tag_references`, so unrecognized keys are logged
  with their full raw path and skipped gracefully instead of crashing.

    concurrent list_tables with configurable DB-level concurrency
    (system_tables_count_db_concurrency setting)

in projection (e.g. information_schema.tables queries)

privilege via has_global_db_table_privilege() → NoCheck strategy,
benefiting both system.tables and system.columns

get_tables, get_ownership, mget_ownerships, list_ownerships)

    | Query   | Before  | After   | Improvement |
    |-------------------|---------|---------|-------------|
    | count(*)          | 1.388s  | 1.017s  | ~27%        |
    | db+table filter   | 0.030s  | 0.013s  | 2.3x        |
    | db-only filter    | 0.020s  | 0.017s  | ~15%        |

* extand optimized path: when filter specifies tables (with or without databases) within reasonable size,

* optimize

commit.message:
refactor(control): extract lua_call helper to deduplicate Lua API wrappers (#19473)

Six async methods in `LuaGrpcClient` and `LuaAdminClient` all shared the
same pattern: call an async API, convert the result to a Lua value, and
return `(Option<Value>, Option<String>)`. The only variation was which API
method was called and the error prefix string.

Extract a generic `lua_call` helper that captures this pattern, reducing
each method body to a single line while preserving the existing error
messages exactly.

Changes:
- Add `lua_call<T, E, F, Fut>` free function handling `lua.to_value` and error formatting
- Replace 6 duplicate `match ... Ok/Err` blocks in `get`, `upsert`, `status`,
  `transfer_leader`, `list_features`, and `set_feature` methods
- Leave `metrics` and `trigger_snapshot` unchanged (different return types)

commit.message:
fix: surface errors silently swallowed in log-storage config and grant entries (#19474)

* fix: surface errors silently swallowed in log-storage config and grant entries

Two places silently discarded errors that could mask misconfiguration or data loss.

1. `cli-config`: `StorageLogConfig::into()` called `.try_into().unwrap_or_default()`
   on log storage params, silently falling back to defaults when params are invalid.
   An operator who configures S3 storage would find the system silently using the
   default (local filesystem) instead. Changed `impl Into<InnerLogHistoryConfig>`
   to `impl TryInto<InnerLogHistoryConfig>` and updated the call site to propagate
   the error.

2. `proto-conv`: `UserGrantSet::from_pb` dropped unrecognized grant entries with
   only a `log::error!`. Added a counter and a `log::warn!` so operators can see
   how many permissions were silently lost during rollback.

* docs: add comments explaining intentional error-handling design

Per reviewer feedback: both cases are by design and should not be changed.

- StorageLogConfig::into(): falling back to default storage params on
  conversion failure is intentional so a misconfigured log-history storage
  does not prevent the node from starting.

- UserGrantSet::from_pb(): silently dropping unrecognized grant entries
  (with error logging) is intentional to keep the node running during
  a version rollback when some grant objects are unrecognizable.

commit.message:
fix(proto-conv): replace silent u64->usize casts with checked conversion (#19471)

* fix(proto-conv): replace silent u64->usize casts with checked conversion

`pool_max_idle_per_host` and `max_concurrent_io_requests` were cast from
`u64` to `usize` with `as`, which silently truncates on 32-bit platforms
when the value exceeds `u32::MAX`.

Replace with `usize::try_from(...).map_err(|_| Incompatible::new(...))` so
the conversion is explicit and returns an error instead of corrupting the
value.

* refactor(storage): return &'static str from storage_type() to avoid allocation

`storage_type()` always returns one of a fixed set of string literals.
Returning `&'static str` instead of `String` eliminates a heap allocation
on every call.

The one caller that passes the result to `UriLocation::new()` (which takes
`String`) now calls `.to_string()` explicitly at the call site.

Changes:
- Change `StorageParams::storage_type()` return type from `String` to `&'static str`
- Add `.to_string()` in `interpreter_table_modify_connection.rs` at the call site

commit.message:
refactor(meta): minor structural cleanups in process, control, admin, semaphore (#19470)

Fix 5 code quality items identified in the meta crate review:

- process/kv_processor.rs: replace `unreachable!()` with `debug_assert!()` to
  avoid panics in release builds; `unreachable!` should be used only when the
  compiler cannot prove a code path is impossible.

- process/process_meta_dir.rs: move the unused `v1_ent` suppression into the
  destructuring pattern (`_v1_ent`) instead of a separate `let _ = v1_ent`
  binding.

- control/export_from_grpc.rs: replace `is_some() + unwrap()` with
  `if let Some(ref mut f)`, eliminating redundant borrows.

- plugins/semaphore/semaphore.rs: initialize `seq_policy` directly with
  `GeneratorKey` instead of first setting `TimeBased` then overwriting it.

- admin/v1/ctrl.rs: add a local `internal_err` helper (matching the one in
  `features.rs`) and replace the three verbose
  `map_err(|e| poem::Error::from_string(e.to_string(), INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR))`
  chains with `.map_err(internal_err)`.

commit.message:
chore: add databend-meta-kv-tests integration test crate for compression (#19467)

test: add databend-meta-kv-tests integration test crate for compression

The existing compression tests in `codec.rs` use a mock KV backend and
only verify encoding in isolation. This new crate writes real data through
`upsert_pb` to a live `MetaStore` and inspects the raw stored bytes,
providing end-to-end confidence that zstd compression is applied on the
actual storage path.

Changes:
- Add `src/meta/kv-tests/` as a new `databend-meta-kv-tests` crate with a
  `[[test]]`-only layout (no lib target)
- `test_large_value_stored_with_compression_header`: upserts a value whose
  proto encoding exceeds `COMPRESS_THRESHOLD`, then reads raw bytes and
  asserts the `[0x0F, 0x01, 0x00, 0x00]` header is present
- `test_small_value_stored_without_compression_header`: same flow with a
  small value, asserts no compression header
- `test_large_value_round_trips_correctly`: writes a large value and reads
  it back via `get_pb`, asserting the decoded value equals the original

commit.message:
feat(meta): transparent zstd compression and typed serialization for meta KV values (#19465)

* feat(meta-api): add transparent zstd compression for protobuf-encoded values

- Add `encode_pb`/`decode_pb` as canonical encode/decode functions in
  `codec.rs`, consolidating duplicated `to_pb + prost::encode` and
  `prost::decode + from_pb` patterns from four write sites and two read sites.

- Add `compress.rs` with transparent zstd compression using a 4-byte magic
  header (`[0x0F, flags1, flags2, flags3]`). Byte 0 is `0x0F` (protobuf
  tag=1 wire-type=7, permanently invalid), unambiguously marking
  custom-encoded values. Values >= 4096 bytes are zstd-compressed; smaller
  values and legacy raw protobuf bytes pass through unchanged (zero copy via
  `Cow::Borrowed`). Unknown flag bits return an error for forward compat.

- Wire `encode_pb`/`decode_pb` through `compress::encode_value`/
  `decode_value`, making compression transparent to all callers.

- Refactor `encode_operation`, `decode_seqv`, `txn_put_pb`, `txn_op_put_pb`,
  `serialize_struct`, and `deserialize_struct` to use the new canonical
  functions. Raw `Vec<u8>` paths (`txn_op_put`) and JSON paths
  (`serialize_u64`) are intentionally untouched.

- Add `zstd` workspace dependency to `databend-common-meta-api`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(meta-process): route all pb encode/decode through the compression API

Replace direct `prost::Message::decode + from_pb` and `to_pb + Message::encode`
call patterns in `pb_value_decoder.rs` and `rewrite_kv.rs` with the canonical
`decode_pb`/`encode_pb` from `databend-common-meta-api`, so compression is
transparent in all meta value encode/decode paths.

- `decode_as<T>`: use `decode_pb` (handles both legacy raw-pb and zstd-compressed)
- `upgrade_table_meta`: use `decode_pb` + `encode_pb` (re-encodes with compression)
- `print_table_meta`: use `decode_pb` (decodes through compression layer before printing)
- Test helper `encode_pb`: delegate to canonical `kv_pb_api::encode_pb`
- Update error-message assertions to match `PbDecodeError` display format
- Add `databend-common-meta-api` dependency to `databend-common-meta-process`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace txn_op_put+serialize_struct with txn_put_pb in api_impl

`serialize_struct()` is a thin wrapper over `encode_pb()` that only
converts the error type. All call sites that paired it with `txn_op_put()`
can be replaced directly with `txn_put_pb()`, which already combines
`encode_pb()` and `TxnOp::put()` internally.

This removes 29 occurrences of the `txn_op_put(&k, serialize_struct(&v)?)`
pattern across six files and replaces them with the more direct
`txn_put_pb(&k, &v)?`. The remaining uses of `serialize_struct` are for
non-put byte serialization (raw `Vec<u8>` returns in index/catalog helpers)
and are left unchanged.

* refactor: add txn_put_u64 and replace txn_op_put+serialize_u64 pattern

`txn_op_put(key, serialize_u64(value)?)` is the u64 equivalent of the
`txn_op_put + serialize_struct` pattern eliminated in the previous commit.
Adding `txn_put_u64(key, value)` as a typed helper keeps the raw-bytes
`txn_op_put` out of call sites and makes the intent clear: name-to-id
mappings stored as JSON-encoded u64.

- Add `txn_put_u64()` to `op_builder.rs`, mirroring `txn_put_pb` in style

* refactor: eliminate raw-bytes txn puts — use typed txn_put_pb everywhere

All `txn_op_put(key, serialize_struct(value)?)` call sites have been
replaced with `txn_put_pb(key, value)?`, which encodes the value
internally. This removes serialization from the application layer and
ensures all KV writes go through the typed protobuf API consistently.

The one remaining raw-bytes case was `txn_op_put(&id_generator, b"")` in
`lock_api.rs`. The ID generator stores no meaningful value — only the KV
seq number is read back. To cover this with the typed API,
`FromToProto for IdGeneratorValue` is implemented using `pb::EmptyProto`
as the wire type, allowing `txn_put_pb` to be used uniformly.

`txn_op_put` is now unused and removed from both the implementation and
the public API. The unused `databend-common-protos` dependency in
`databend-common-meta-process` is also removed.

Changes:
- Add `impl FromToProto for IdGeneratorValue` in `proto-conv/src/impls/id.rs`
- Remove `txn_op_put` from `op_builder.rs` and `lib.rs`

* refactor: rename txn builder functions for consistency

Rename the txn op builder functions to use a consistent `txn_` prefix
without the redundant `op_` infix, and clarify the TTL variant with a
`_with_ttl` suffix:

- `txn_op_put_pb`  → `txn_put_pb_with_ttl`
- `txn_op_get`     → `txn_get`
- `txn_op_del`     → `txn_del`

`txn_put_pb` now delegates to `txn_put_pb_with_ttl(key, value, None)`,
removing the duplicated `encode_pb` call that previously existed between
the two put functions.

* refactor: replace upsert_kv with upsert_pb across management and API layers

Raw `upsert_kv` calls require manual serialization to `Vec<u8>` before writing
and raw deserialization after reading, bypassing type safety and the compression
layer added for protobuf-encoded values. Replacing them with `upsert_pb` lets
the typed API handle encoding transparently.

Changes:
- Specialize `fetch_id` from `<T: kvapi::Key>` to concrete `IdGenerator`,
  replacing the empty-bytes `upsert_kv` with `upsert_pb(IdGeneratorValue)`
- Replace `serialize_struct + upsert_kv(Update)` in `upsert_role_info` with `upsert_pb`
- Replace all `upsert_kv(Delete)` calls in `drop_role`, `drop_user`,
  `drop_token`, and `drop_client_session_id` with `UpsertPB::delete`
- Replace `UpsertKV::delete` calls in test helpers with `UpsertPB::delete`

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

the typed API handle encoding transparently.

Changes:
- Specialize `fetch_id` from `<T: kvapi::Key>` to concrete `IdGenerator`,
  replacing the empty-bytes `upsert_kv` with `upsert_pb(IdGeneratorValue)`
- Replace `serialize_struct + upsert_kv(Update)` in `upsert_role_info` with `upsert_pb`
- Replace all `upsert_kv(Delete)` calls in `drop_role`, `drop_user`,
  `drop_token`, and `drop_client_session_id` with `UpsertPB::delete`
- Replace `UpsertKV::delete` calls in test helpers with `UpsertPB::delete`

commit.message:
refactor: move meta plugin crates and eliminate unnecessary clones in UserGrantSet (#19466)

* refactor: move semaphore and cache into src/meta/plugins/ and rename crates

These two crates are higher-level components built on top of
`databend-meta-client`, not core meta infrastructure. Grouping them
under `src/meta/plugins/` makes their role as optional, independent
extensions explicit. The rename from `databend-common-meta-*` to
`databend-meta-plugin-*` drops the `common` prefix (they are not
general shared infrastructure) and adds `plugin` to signal that they
are layered additions rather than foundational crates.

Changes:
- Move `src/meta/semaphore/` → `src/meta/plugins/semaphore/`
- Move `src/meta/cache/` → `src/meta/plugins/cache/`
- Rename crate `databend-common-meta-semaphore` → `databend-meta-plugin-semaphore`
- Rename crate `databend-common-meta-cache` → `databend-meta-plugin-cache`
- Update workspace `Cargo.toml` members, default-members, and dependency entries
- Update all dependent crates and `use` statements in Rust source files

* refactor: eliminate unnecessary clones in UserGrantSet and related code

`UserGrantSet::entries()` previously cloned the entire entries vector on
each call, and `roles()` did the same for the roles set. Returning
references instead avoids these allocations and makes the ownership
intent clearer to callers — they observe state, not take ownership.
`SnapshotRefType` gains `Copy` so it no longer needs `.clone()` at use
sites.

Changes:
- Change `entries()` return type from `Vec<GrantEntry>` to `&[GrantEntry]`
- Change `roles()` return type from `Vec<String>` to `&HashSet<String>`
- Derive `Copy` on `SnapshotRefType` (two-variant C-like enum)
- Use `into_iter()` instead of `iter()` in proto-conv `from_pb` to avoid
  cloning entries during deserialization

* refactor: add `roles_vec()` to `UserGrantSet` to simplify callers

Callers that needed a `Vec<String>` of the granted roles had to write
`.roles().iter().cloned().collect()` everywhere, which is noisy and
distracts from the actual intent. `roles_vec()` encapsulates the
conversion so each call site expresses only what it needs.

commit.message:
refactor: clean up meta crates — simplify Cargo.toml, remove unused deps, and deduplicate code (#19462)

* refactor: rename `decode_transition` to `decode_change`

The function decodes a `Change<Vec<u8>>` into `Change<T>`, so the name
should match the type it operates on. The parameter name `transition`
is also renamed to `change` for consistency.

* refactor: extract shared HTTP request logic in MetaAdminClient

Every method in `MetaAdminClient` duplicated the same
send-request/check-status/parse-or-error pattern. Extract this into two
helpers: `checked_get()` for status-checked raw responses and
`get_json()` for JSON deserialization, eliminating the repetition.

* refactor: deduplicate chunking logic in `get_pb_values_vec`

`get_pb_values_vec` and `get_pb_vec` had identical chunking logic.
Since `get_pb_values_vec` is just `get_pb_vec` with keys stripped
(both ultimately call `get_pb_stream_low`), rewrite it to delegate
to `get_pb_vec` instead of duplicating the chunk-and-collect loop.

* refactor: simplify `KVPbApi` trait methods with idiomatic patterns

Several methods in `KVPbApi` used verbose patterns where idiomatic Rust
combinators express the same intent more concisely:

- `get_pb_values` now delegates to `get_pb_stream` (which already wraps
  errors) instead of duplicating the error conversion from
  `get_pb_stream_low`
- `get_pb_stream` uses `map_ok` instead of manual `match Ok/Err`
- `get_pb_stream_low` uses `.map().transpose()` instead of
  `if let Some / else None`
- Remove unnecessary `key.clone()` in `get_pb_low`
- Remove duplicate TODO comment

* refactor: extract `assert_table_id_is_latest` to deduplicate table ID validation

The same 10-line check — verify that a table ID matches the last entry
in the table ID history list — was duplicated three times across
`rename_table` and `swap_table`. Extract it into a shared helper that
takes the operation name for error context.

* chore: remove `kvapi-tests` crate from workspace

The `databend-common-meta-kvapi-tests` crate contained integration tests
for `kvapi` against metasrv, delegating to `databend-meta-kvapi-test-suite`
for the actual test logic. These tests are now covered elsewhere, so the
crate is removed to reduce workspace size.

Changes:
- Remove `src/meta/kvapi-tests` from workspace `members` and `default-members`
- Remove `databend-common-meta-kvapi-tests` from `[workspace.dependencies]`

* chore: remove unused external crates from workspace dependencies

Audit identified 23 external crates declared in `[workspace.dependencies]`
that are never referenced by any workspace member. Removing them reduces
noise and prevents stale version pins from accumulating over time.

* chore: remove unused dev-dependencies from meta crates

These dev-dependencies were declared but never referenced in any test
source file, confirmed by text search across all `tests/` and `src/`
paths in the affected crates.

Changes:
- Remove `databend-meta-kvapi` from `cache` and `semaphore` dev-deps
- Remove `env_logger`, `regex`, `temp-env`, `tempfile` from `admin` dev-deps
- Remove `databend-common-version` from `store` dev-deps

* chore: simplify Cargo.toml in src/meta/* crates

Remove noise from `Cargo.toml` files across the meta crates. Deps already
listed in `[dependencies]` are available in tests and benchmarks without
re-declaration in `[dev-dependencies]`.

Changes:
- Remove empty sections (`[dependencies]`, `[dev-dependencies]`, `[build-dependencies]`)
- Remove boilerplate cargo comment ("See more keys and their definitions...")
- Remove redundant `test = true` from `[lib]` (it is the default)
- Drop dev-dep entries that duplicate a regular dep: `databend-meta-types`,
  `databend-meta-runtime-api`, `databend-meta`, `log`, `futures`, `tokio`,
  `anyhow` across `admin`, `cache`, `control`, and `semaphore`

* chore: fix taplo formatting in src/meta/store/Cargo.toml

commit.message:
refactor: merge external-meta-version into MetaClient/MetaService columns (#19460)

The single `external-meta-version` column cannot represent diverged CalVer
versions for `databend-meta-client` and `databend-meta` service independently.
This replaces the 4-column format with a 3-column format where CalVer values
go directly into the MetaClient/MetaService columns with an `ext:` prefix
(e.g., `ext:260205.0.0`), making per-column resolution explicit.

Data cleanup: removed 136 rows with `-` placeholder values, stripped trailing
`-` and `-n` suffixes from old tags. Repeated column headers every 40 rows
for scrollability.

Changes:
- Add `ExternalMinCompatibles` class encapsulating CalVer-to-tag resolution
  with per-column mapping (same CalVer in different columns maps to different
  first tags)
- Extract shared `parse_tsv()` into `tsv.py` to deduplicate between scripts
- Merge duplicate `query_compat`/`metasrv_compat` into parameterized
  `print_version_compat`; same for `all_*` variants
- Standardize column order: client in column 2 across input, resolved output,
  and `find_compat.py`

commit.message:
refactor: extract fuse compat test matrix into test_cases.yaml (#19441)

* refactor: rewrite test_compat_fuse

* refactor: extract fuse compat test matrix into test_cases.yaml

* docs: update compat_fuse README to match current implementation

* refactor: merge compat-stateless flashback tests into parameterized script and wire into CI

* fix: correct NEW_FMT_VER for v2 case and document stateless field in README

* fix: use current meta for reader phase in meta compression cases

* refactor: replace hand-rolled yaml parser with PyYAML

* fix: add intermediate meta upgrade steps for meta compression cases

* fix: restore original meta chains and download reader binary

* fix: bump meta versions to satisfy current query min-compatible-metasrv-version

* fix: remove V0 compat tests (1.0.56/1.1.30) dropped since ea91e06b93

* fix: add .test extension to sqllogictest files so runner actually executes them

The sqllogictest runner filters files by `.test` extension
(tests/sqllogictests/src/main.rs:308). Without it, `--run_file` never
matches and 0 tests are executed. This is a pre-existing bug on main
that affects all compat_fuse cases.

* fix: remove trailing commas in base/fuse_compat_write.test

Old query (1.2.46) does not support trailing commas in CREATE TABLE
column definitions or INSERT VALUES. This was never caught because
the test files were never actually executed (missing .test extension).

* fix: update expected show grants output in revoke/fuse_compat_read

Current query expands ALL into individual privilege names
(CREATE,SELECT,INSERT,...) instead of showing ALL.

* fix: split rbac tests into basic (1.2.311) and full (1.2.833) suites

rbac/ reverted to pre-54fceaa6b0 content compatible with writer 1.2.311.
rbac_all/ preserves the expanded test (procedure/sequence/connection/
warehouse) with writer 1.2.833 which supports all these features.

* fix: typo in rbac_all read test - access procedure, not access sequence

PR #18730 wrote "GRANT access sequence on procedure" which is
syntactically wrong. Should be "GRANT access procedure on procedure".
Never caught because the test was never executed.

* refactor: rename rbac test suites to include version context

rbac -> rbac_v1_2_311 (compatible with writer 1.2.311/1.2.318)
rbac_all -> rbac_current (requires current query features)

* fix: suppress noisy CI output from git advice and old binary backtraces

- Add -c advice.detachedHead=false to git clone in git_partial_clone()
- Capture --cmd ver output to suppress backtrace from old binaries

* refactor: improve version check output in CI logs

- Add context message before/after version checks
- Only print the "version:" line from --cmd ver output
- Use "checking metasrv/query" prefix for clarity

* fix: lazy-import PyYAML so single-case mode works without it

* docs: clarify procedure version comment in test_cases.yaml

* docs: fix sqllogictest filenames in README to include .test extension

* docs: fix docstring to cover both backward and forward compat

* fix: use explicit privilege list instead of ALL in revoke write test

commit.message:
refactor: reorganize meta/api into submodules (#19454)

The `src/meta/api/src/` crate had 43 files flat at root level — a mix of
domain APIs, implementations, and utilities. Reorganize into 5 submodules
(`api_impl/`, `txn/`, `error/`, `kv/`, `util/`) for better navigability.

All external paths are preserved via backward-compat `pub use` aliases in
`lib.rs`, so no changes are needed outside this crate. Intra-module
references in `api_impl/` updated from `crate::` to `super::` for sibling
modules.

Changes:
- `txn/`: 6 files (`txn_backoff.rs` → `backoff.rs`, `reply.rs`, etc.)
- `error/`: 3 files (`kv_app_error.rs` → `app_error.rs`, etc.)
- `kv/`: 4 entries (`kv_pb_api/` → `pb_api/`, `crud/`, etc.)
- `util/`: 2 files (`util.rs` → `idempotent_sender.rs`, etc.)
- `api_impl/`: 26 domain API files (filenames unchanged)

commit.message:
refactor: meta/proto-conv: simplify conversions and reorganize into submodules (#19453)

* refactor: bound retry loops and remove unused `to_nonempty`

`bench_client_num_conn` had an infinite loop that never returned despite
its `Result<()>` signature. Replace with a bounded `for` loop controlled
by a new `--num` CLI flag (default 10000).

In `register_node`, when the leader node is not yet replicated locally,
the code slept but fell through to attempt registration anyway (wasted
attempt). Add `continue` so it waits properly before retrying.

Remove unused `Tenant::to_nonempty()` which contained an `.unwrap()` on
a fallible conversion.

Changes:
- Add `--num` flag to `BenchArgs` to control bench iteration count
- Add `continue` after sleep in `register_node` leader-not-replicated path
- Remove `Tenant::to_nonempty()`

* refactor: replace `.unwrap()` with proper error handling in `TableInfo::database_name`

The `desc` field has format `'db_name'.'table_name'`, and the old code
used `.unwrap()` on `split('.').next()` and unchecked `[1..len-1]`
slicing to strip quotes. Both panic if `desc` is empty or malformed.

Replace with `strip_prefix`/`strip_suffix` which return `Result` with
descriptive error messages including the table name for diagnosis.

* refactor: meta/proto-conv: simplify conversions and reorganize into submodules

Replace verbose `match Some/None` patterns with a new `ToProtoOptionExt`

commit.message:
feat: add --decode-values flag to metactl dump-raft-log-wal (#19448)

* feat: add --decode-values flag to metactl dump-raft-log-wal

Previously, `dump-raft-log-wal` displayed protobuf-encoded values as raw
bytes (e.g., `Update("[binary]")`). The new `--decode-values` flag uses
key prefixes (`__fd_database_by_id`, `__fd_roles`, etc.) to route values
to their protobuf types via `FromToProto`, rendering them as debug
structs (`DatabaseMeta { engine: "", ... }`).

The decoder is in `meta/process` (which already has all proto-conv deps)
while the dump logic is in `meta/control` (alongside other metactl
utilities), keeping `meta/binaries` as thin CLI dispatch.

Example:

    databend-metactl dump-raft-log-wal --raft-dir .databend/meta1 --decode-values

    RaftLog:
    ChunkId(00_000_000_000_000_000_000)
      R-00015: ... Append(... txn:TxnRequest{if:[...] then:[Put(Put key=__fd_database_by_id/1),...] else:[]})
        txn.if_then[0].put __fd_database_by_id/1:
          DatabaseMeta { engine: "", engine_options: {}, options: {}, ... }
        txn.if_then[1].put __fd_db_id_list/test_tenant/default:
          DbIdList { id_list: [1] }
      R-00017: ... Append(... txn:TxnRequest{if:[...] then:[Put(Put key=__fd_roles/test_tenant/public)] else:[...]})
        txn.if_then[0].put __fd_roles/test_tenant/public:
          RoleInfo { name: "public", grants: UserGrantSet { entries: [], roles: {} }, ... }

Changes:
- Add `pb_value_decoder` module with `decode_pb_value()` (30 key-prefix
  mappings) and `decode_cmd_values()` for `UpsertKV`/`Transaction` cmds
- Add `dump_raft_log_wal` module using `Dump::write_with()` to append
  decoded lines after each `Append(Normal(...))` WAL record
- Add static WAL integration test with a real 283KB WAL fixture
  containing `__fd_*` keys that decode to actual protobuf types

* chore: add trailing slash to prefix matching

* fix: use fixed timestamp in WAL decode test for cross-platform determinism

`DatabaseMeta::default()` calls `Utc::now()`, whose protobuf varint
encoding size varies with the timestamp value (e.g., 214 bytes locally
vs 220 on CI). Replace with an explicit construction using a fixed
timestamp (`2024-01-01T00:00:00Z`) so the encoded size is deterministic
(200 bytes) across all platforms.

Also remove the now-unused `normalize_timestamps` helper and `regex`
dev-dependency since the fixed timestamp makes masking unnecessary.

* feat: add --raw flag to metactl dump-raft-log-wal

When debugging cross-platform protobuf encoding differences, the
`--decode-values` flag shows decoded structs but hides the actual byte
encoding. The new `--raw` / `-R` flag dumps raw protobuf byte arrays
so you can see exactly what bytes differ. Both flags can be combined.

Changes:
- Add `raw_cmd_values()` and `raw_txn_op()` parallel to their decode
  counterparts, formatting bytes as `[10, 0, 26, ...]`
- Thread `raw` bool through `dump_wal()`, collecting extra lines before
  consuming the WAL record to avoid borrow conflicts

Example:

    metactl dump-raft-log-wal --raft-dir .databend/meta --raw

      R-00001: [... Size(200): Append(...)
        raw(__fd_database_by_id/123): [10, 0, 26, 12, ...]

    metactl dump-raft-log-wal --raft-dir .databend/meta -V -R

      R-00001: [...) Size(200): Append(...)
        value(__fd_database_by_id/123): DatabaseMeta { engine: , ... }
        raw(__fd_database_by_id/123): [10, 0, 26, 12, ...]
EOF
)

commit.message:
fix: upgrade databend-meta crates to v260205.3.0 (#19438)

* fix: upgrade databend-meta crates to v260205.3.0

This upgrade includes raft-log 0.3.0 bug fixes, a committed-log-id
flush fix, and a re-export refactor that removes the need for a direct
`raft-log` dependency.

raft-log 0.3.0 bug fixes:
- Chunk rotation sent wrong file to FlushWorker; new chunk was never fsync'd.

raft-log 0.3.0 refactoring:
- Data writes moved into FlushWorker for batched I/O.
- Replaced `seek+read` with `pread` to prevent race conditions.
- `flush(callback)` now takes `Option<T::Callback>` for best-effort writes.

databend-meta fixes:
- `save_committed` now issues a fire-and-forget `flush(None)` to persist
  committed log id on a best-effort basis.

databend-meta refactoring:
- Re-export `raft_log` from `databend-meta-raft-store`, removing the need
  for downstream crates to depend on `raft-log` directly.

Changes:
- Remove direct `raft-log` dependency from workspace, `databend-meta-binaries`,
  and `databend-common-meta-control`
- Use `databend_meta_raft_store::raft_log::` re-export path in `import.rs`
  and `metactl/main.rs`
- Update compat docs with versions 1.2.881 and 1.2.882

* chore fix lint

* docs: fix broken links in meta and common READMEs

After the meta-service refactoring in v1.2.874 that moved core implementation
to the external databend-meta repository, several README links became stale.
This fixes the broken links reported by the link checker (issue #19412).

Changes:
- Remove references to moved core/ directories (client, raft-store, types, etc.)
- Update kvapi-test-suite to kvapi-tests (renamed directory)
- Move core/store reference to top-level store
- Remove non-existent auth directory from common README
- Add link to external databend-meta repository

- Fix: #19412

commit.message:
refactor: replace in-tree meta core crates with external databend-meta v260205.1.0

* refactor: replace in-tree meta core crates with external databend-meta v260205.1.0

The meta core crates (`src/meta/core/`) — service, client, kvapi, raft-store,
sled-store, types, runtime-api, and test-harness — are removed from the monorepo
and replaced with the published external `databend-meta` crate family at
v260205.1.0. This completes the separation of the meta-service into its own
repository (databendlabs/databend-meta), reducing build coupling and enabling
independent release cycles.

All internal crate references are updated from `databend-common-meta-*` path
dependencies to the external `databend-meta-*` crates via `[patch.crates-io]`
git tag overrides. A new `databend-meta-ver` crate provides user-facing SemVer
compatibility constants for `--cmd ver` display, decoupled from the CalVer
protocol negotiation versions used internally by the external crate.

Changes:
- Delete `src/meta/core/` (service, client, kvapi, raft-store, sled-store,
  types, runtime-api, test-harness, kvapi-test-suite)
- Add `databend-meta-ver` crate with `MIN_METASRV_VER_FOR_QUERY` and
  `MIN_QUERY_VER_FOR_METASRV` for user-facing version display
- Remove version argument from `GrpcServer::create` calls (upstream API change)
- Upgrade `databend-base` from v0.2.9 to v0.2.10

* feat: add query-meta version compatibility documentation

Add `compat_docs/` with a 3-stage pipeline (extract → resolve → query)
that builds compatible version range tables between databend-query and
databend-meta. Source data is extracted from git tags, CalVer references
are resolved to repo tags via external crate mappings, and the final
output lists compatible version ranges for every release.

Changes:
- Add `extract_from_git.py` to extract min-compatible versions from git history
- Add `resolve_versions.py` to merge external crate versions and resolve CalVer to SemVer
- Add `find_compat.py` to query or enumerate compatible version ranges
- Add pre-generated `query_to_meta.txt` and `meta_to_query.txt` lookup tables

* chore: remove nextest config for removed databend-meta crate

* M  src/meta/ver/src/lib.rs

* refactor: move compat_docs into src/meta/ver

The compatibility documentation now lives alongside the version
crate it documents, keeping version-related artifacts co-located
under `src/meta/ver/`.

commit.message:
fix: upgrade raft-log: use pread to prevent race condition in concurrent chunk reads (#19401)

Chunk::read_record() had a race condition when multiple threads read
from the same chunk concurrently. The non-atomic seek + read operation
allowed one thread's seek to be overwritten by another before reading,
causing reads from wrong file positions.

Changes:
- Use read_exact_at() (pread) instead of seek() + read() in read_record()
- Add concurrent read test to verify the fix

commit.message:
refactor: unify gRPC message size into single config value (#19397)

* refactor: unify gRPC message size into single config value

Consolidate `grpc_max_encoding_size` and `grpc_max_decoding_size` into
a single `grpc_max_message_size` parameter since they are always set
together. Also change the parameter type from `Option<usize>` to `usize`
to require explicit values at construction time.

Changes:
- Unify encoding/decoding size into `grpc_max_message_size` in `RaftConfig` and `GrpcConfig`
- Change `RpcClientConf.grpc_max_message_size` from `Option<usize>` to `usize`
- Change `MetaGrpcClient::try_create()` and `try_create_with_features()` params to `usize`
- Export `DEFAULT_GRPC_MESSAGE_SIZE` from `databend-common-meta-client`
- Update all call sites to use `DEFAULT_GRPC_MESSAGE_SIZE` explicitly
- Remove unused dependencies: `chrono`, `chrono-tz` from `databend-common-io`, `databend-common-column` from `databend-common-hashtable`, `chrono-tz` from `databend-common-formats`

Config example (databend-meta):

```toml
# MetaService gRPC message size (default: 32MB)
grpc_api_max_message_size = 67108864  # 64MB

[raft_config]
# Raft gRPC message size (default: 32MB)
raft_grpc_max_message_size = 67108864  # 64MB
```

Config example (databend-query):

```toml
[meta]
# Meta client gRPC message size (default: 32MB)
grpc_max_message_size = 67108864  # 64MB
```

commit.message:
refactor: add connect method to SpawnApi for channel creation (#19370)

* refactor: improve dns_resolver module structure and documentation

Refactor `ConnectionFactory` methods to use early returns and extract
independent logic into focused helper functions. Add comprehensive
documentation to all public and private types.

Changes:
- Extract `build_uri()` for URI construction with scheme selection
- Extract `apply_tls_config()` for TLS endpoint configuration
- Refactor `create_rpc_endpoint()` to use linear flow with early returns
- Add doc comments to all structs, fields, and functions
- Make `create_rpc_endpoint()` private
- Remove unused `databend-common-base` dev-dependency from client crate

* refactor: add connect method to SpawnApi for channel creation

Decouple `channel_manager.rs` from direct `ConnectionFactory` dependency
by adding `SpawnApi::connect()` method. This allows client tests to use
`TokioRuntime` without pulling in the full Databend runtime infrastructure.

Changes:
- Add `TlsConfig` and `ChannelError` types to runtime-api
- Add `SpawnApi::connect()` method for gRPC channel creation
- Implement `connect()` in `TokioRuntime` with TLS support
- Implement `connect()` in `DatabendRuntime` using `ConnectionFactory`
- Update `MetaChannelManager` to use generic `R: SpawnApi`

* fix: set per-RPC timeout in TokioRuntime::connect

Add `endpoint.timeout(t)` in addition to `connect_timeout(t)` to enforce
per-RPC timeouts. Without this, clients using `TokioRuntime` would only
have connection handshake timeouts, allowing slow RPCs to run indefinitely.

`databend-common-meta-client` to complete the decoupling of the gRPC

chore: fix lint (#19363)

* refactor: decouple ClientWorkerRequest from TrackingPayload

Replace the direct `TrackingPayload` field with a type-erased closure that
encapsulates the tracking logic. This removes the dependency on tracking
types from the message module.

Changes:
- Replace `tracking_payload` field with `tracking_fn` closure in `ClientWorkerRequest`
- Remove `TrackingPayload` import from `message.rs`
- Remove unused `ThreadTracker` import from `grpc_client.rs`

* refactor: add capture_tracking_context() to SpawnApi trait

Abstract the ThreadTracker context capture into SpawnApi, removing the
direct dependency on databend-common-base from the meta client crate.
This continues decoupling meta-client from query-specific infrastructure.

Changes:
- Add `capture_tracking_context()` to `SpawnApi` trait
- Implement no-op version for `TokioRuntime`
- Implement `ThreadTracker`-based version for `DatabendRuntime`
- Remove `databend-common-base` and `databend-common-metrics` dependencies from client crate

* refactor: remove databend-common-meta-app-types crate

Upgrade `databend-base` to 0.2.7 and remove the `databend-common-meta-app-types`
crate, since it only re-exported `NonEmptyStr`/`NonEmptyString` which are now
available directly in `databend-base`.

Changes:
- Upgrade `databend-base` from 0.2.5 to 0.2.7
- Delete `databend-common-meta-app-types` crate
- Update imports to use `databend_base::non_empty::NonEmptyString`
- Remove `databend-common-meta-app-types` dependency from consumer crates

* refactor: move KVApi implementation from ClientHandle to MetaStore

Decouple `ClientHandle` from `kvapi` dependency by moving `KVApi` trait
implementation to `MetaStore`. This keeps `ClientHandle` as a pure gRPC
communication layer without business logic dependencies.

Changes:
- Remove `KVApi` impl from `ClientHandle` (delete `kv_api_impl.rs`)
- Add `KVApi` impl for `MetaStore` in `databend-common-meta-store`
- Replace `Deref` impl on `MetaStore` with explicit `inner()` method
- Add `watch()` method to `MetaStore` for watch stream access
- Update `metabench` to wrap `ClientHandle` in `MetaStore`
- Update test builders in `kvapi-tests` and `schema-api-tests` to return `MetaStore`
- Replace `.deref()` calls with `.inner()` across codebase

* chore: fix lint

* chore: fix lint

chore: fix lint (#19361)

* refactor: clean up imports in test harness

Reduce top-level imports from `databend_common_tracing` by using fully
qualified paths and adding a local `closure_name()` helper function.

Changes:
- Remove `databend_common_tracing` imports, use fully qualified paths
- Extract `init_logging()` into separate function
- Add local `closure_name()` function

* refactor: use tokio::spawn directly in client test

Replace `databend_common_base::runtime::spawn` with `tokio::spawn` in
the test file to reduce unnecessary dependency on `databend_common_base`.

Changes:
- Replace `databend_common_base::runtime::spawn` with `tokio::spawn`

* refactor: make metrics statics private in grpc_metrics

Make the metric static variables private since they are only accessed
through the public `record_*` functions.

Changes:
- Remove `pub` from metric static variables

* refactor: abstract gRPC client metrics into ClientMetricsApi trait

Move client metrics from hardcoded implementation to a trait-based abstraction,
allowing different runtimes to provide different metrics behavior. `TokioRuntime`
uses `NoopMetrics` for lightweight/testing scenarios, while `DatabendRuntime`
uses `DatabendMetrics` with real Prometheus metrics.

Changes:
- Add `ClientMetricsApi` trait with `record_request_duration()`, `request_inflight()`, `record_request_success()`, `record_request_failed()`, and `record_make_client_fail()`
- Add `type ClientMetrics: ClientMetricsApi` associated type to `SpawnApi`
- Add `NoopMetrics` implementation for `TokioRuntime`
- Move `grpc_metrics.rs` to `runtime/src/metrics.rs` as `DatabendMetrics`
- Update client code to use `RT::ClientMetrics::*` instead of direct metrics calls

* chore: fix lint

☁️ Try Cloud • 🚀 Quick Start • 📖 Documentation • 💬 Slack

💡 Why Databend?

Databend is an open-source enterprise data warehouse built in Rust.

Core capabilities: Analytics, vector search, full-text search, auto schema evolution — unified in one engine.

Agent-ready: Sandbox UDFs for agent logic, SQL for orchestration, transactions for reliability, branching for safe experimentation on production data.
📊 Core EngineAnalytics, vector search, full-text search, auto schema evolution, transactions. 🤖 Agent-ReadySandbox UDF + SQL orchestration. Build and run agents on your enterprise data.
🏢 Enterprise ScaleElastic compute, cloud native. S3/Azure/GCS. 🌿 BranchingGit-like data versioning. Agents safely operate on production snapshots.

⚡ Quick Start

1. Cloud (Recommended)

Start for free on Databend Cloud — Production-ready in 60 seconds.

2. Local (Python)

Ideal for development and testing:

pip install databend

import databend
ctx = databend.SessionContext()
ctx.sql("SELECT 'Hello, Databend!'").show()

3. Docker

Run the full warehouse locally:

docker run -p 8000:8000 datafuselabs/databend

🤖 Agent-Ready Architecture

Databend's Sandbox UDF enables flexible agent orchestration with a three-layer architecture:

  • Control Plane: Resource scheduling, permission validation, sandbox lifecycle management
  • Execution Plane (Databend): SQL orchestration, issues requests via Arrow Flight
  • Compute Plane (Sandbox Workers): Isolated sandboxes running your agent logic
-- Define your agent logic
CREATE FUNCTION my_agent(input STRING) RETURNS STRING
LANGUAGE python HANDLER = 'run'
AS $$
def run(input):
    # Your agent logic: LLM calls, tool use, reasoning...
    return response
$$;

-- Orchestrate agents with SQL
SELECT my_agent(question) FROM tasks;

🚀 Use Cases

  • AI Agents: Sandbox UDF + SQL orchestration + branching for safe operations
  • Analytics & BI: Large-scale SQL analytics — Learn more
  • Search & RAG: Vector + full-text search — Learn more
